questions.question: Which is the world’s largest government-funded health insurance scheme that completed seven years on September 23, 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana

questions.explanation: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) completes seven years since its launch on September 23, 2018 from Ranchi. It is the world’s largest government-funded health assurance scheme, covering over 55 crore beneficiaries. It provides ₹5 lakh annual health cover per family for cashless and paperless treatment across India. The scheme is described as a people’s movement transforming India’s public healthcare, ensuring top-quality and affordable medical services.

questions.question: What is the name of the portal launched on National Ayurveda Day for digitized data on Ayurvedic ingredients and products?
questions.explanation: The DRAVYA portal (Digitized Retrieval Application for Versatile Yardstick of AYUSH Substances) was launched on National Ayurveda Day, September 23, 2025. It is an initiative of the Ministry of Ayush. The portal is the largest publicly accessible collection of data on Ayurvedic ingredients and products. It combines information from classical Ayurveda textbooks, contemporary scientific literature, and field studies. DRAVYA is an ever-growing and evolving database, providing researchers, practitioners, and the public with reliable, comprehensive, and easily accessible knowledge. The portal strengthens Ayurveda research, standardization, and global outreach.

questions.question: The Sujalam Bharat Summit is a national initiative led by which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Central Water Commission (CWC) under the Ministry of Jal Shakti organised a virtual workshop on “Technology for Efficient Water Management” in the Sujalam Bharat Summit 2025. The Sujalam Bharat Summit is a national initiative led by the Ministry of Jal Shakti and coordinated by NITI Aayog. It focuses on water management, sanitation, and sustainable practices. The summit aims to bring grassroots perspectives into national policy-making in line with the Prime Minister’s vision.

questions.question: ECINET is a unified digital platform developed by which government body to streamline and simplify electoral processes?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) launched a new e-sign feature on its ECINET portal and app. The feature allows citizens to verify their identity using Aadhaar-linked phone numbers. ECINET is a unified digital platform by the ECI to streamline and simplify electoral processes. It serves as a single-point interface for all election-related services. The platform integrates over 40 existing mobile and web applications, including Voter Helpline App, cVIGIL, and Suvidha 2.0. The new e-sign feature enhances digital verification, simplifies voter services, and strengthens secure access on ECINET.

questions.question: What is the theme of World Environmental Health Day 2025?
questions.explanation: Every year on 26th September, World Environmental Health Day (WEHD) is observed globally, led by the International Federation of Environmental Health (IFEH). In 2025, the theme is “Clean Air, Healthy People”, highlighting the role of air quality in public health. The aim is to spread awareness and push for action that clean air is essential for human well-being, not just an environmental issue. The focus is especially urgent for India, which faces one of the world’s highest burdens of indoor air pollution (IAP).

questions.question: The International Day for the Total Elimination of Nuclear Weapons is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: The United Nations (UN) observes the International Day for the Total Elimination of Nuclear Weapons every year on 26th September. The day raises awareness about nuclear dangers and promotes complete elimination of nuclear weapons. The first celebration of the day was on 26th September 2014. As of 2025, approximately 12,241 nuclear weapons exist worldwide. Countries with nuclear weapons are investing in well-funded, long-term modernization of their arsenals. More than 50% of the global population lives in countries that possess nuclear weapons or are part of nuclear alliances.

questions.question: Where was the 12th Sustainable Mountain Development Summit held in India?
questions.explanation: The 12th Sustainable Mountain Development Summit (SMDS-XII) was held at Doon University, Dehradun on 26 September. Sustainable Mountain Development Summit is a flagship annual summit addressing ecological, economic, and social challenges of the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR). It is organised by the Integrated Mountain Initiative (IMI), a civil society-led network. The aim is to promote sustainable Himalayan development, strengthen community participation, and blend science with traditional wisdom.

questions.question: Which protocol led to the creation of Central American Integration System (SICA)?
questions.explanation: India deepened engagement with Central American Integration System (SICA) countries at the India-SICA Foreign Ministers’ Meeting in New York. Key collaboration areas include trade, digital payments, agriculture, renewable energy, pharmaceuticals, and information technology (IT). Central American Integration System (SICA) is the institutional framework for regional integration in Central America. It was established in 1991 through the Tegucigalpa Protocol by amending the Charter of the Organization of Central American States (ODECA) of 1962.

questions.question: Which country won the Asia Cricket Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: On 28 September 2025, India won the Asia Cricket Cup 2025 in the Men’s T20 format against Pakistan. This is India’s second Asia Cup T20 title after 2016. Overall, India has now won the Asia Cup 9 times in the 17th edition of the tournament. Tilak Varma played a match-winning unbeaten 69 runs, rebuilding the innings under intense pressure.

questions.question: The Armed Forces (Special Powers) Act (AFSPA) was enacted by the Parliament in which year?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) extended the Armed Forces (Special Powers) Act (AFSPA), 1958 for six months in parts of Manipur, Arunachal Pradesh, and Nagaland on 26 September 2025. The Armed Forces (Special Powers) Act (AFSPA) was enacted by the Parliament in 1958. The Act was passed to provide special powers to armed forces in “disturbed areas” to combat insurgency and maintain public order where civilian authorities were unable to function effectively.
